title: NeuroVoices: Chitra Venkatasubramanian, MBBS, MD, MSc, FNCS, on Debating Optimal Blood Pressure Targets After Intracerebral Hemorrhage
summary: The clinical professor of stroke neurology at Stanford University discussed the clinical rationale and evidence supporting early, intensive blood pressure lowering in patients with intracerebral hemorrhage.

title: Neural organoids offer insight into mechanisms of dementia
summary: Researchers at The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center and College of Medicine have discovered a new way that neurons act in neurodegeneration by using human neural organoids – also known as "mini-brain" models – from patients with frontotemporal lobar degeneration (FTLD).

title: Rising Skin Cancer Rates: Epidemic or Overdiagnosis?
summary: While skin cancer rates are growing in many parts of the world, experts disagree on whether this represents a true epidemic or overdiagnosis is a contributing factor.

title: Critically ill patients in African hospitals aren’t getting the care they need: new survey
summary: A study found 69% of critically ill patients in African countries were treated in general hospital wards.

title: Magnetic Brain Stimulation Shows Promise in Alzheimer's Care
summary: Personalised magnetic stimulation of the precuneus over 52 weeks slows cognitive and functional decline compared with sham in mild to moderate Alzheimer's, a trial shows.

title: Chemotherapy linked to changes in brain connectivity in breast cancer patients
summary: New research published in the Journal of Magnetic Resonance Imaging has uncovered changes in brain connectivity during chemotherapy in patients with breast cancer.

title: Continuous Ketone Monitoring: Essential or Optional?
summary: Two endocrinologists who live with type 1 diabetes debated this issue at the Advanced Technologies & Treatments for Diabetes (ATTD) 2025 meeting.

title: Primary Care Primer for Headache Management, with Morris Levin, MD
summary: ACP 2025. The director of the UCSF Headache Center built an evidence-based framework for diagnosing and treating headache disorders seen in primary care.

title: Chemotherapy has a hidden impact on the brain we did not know about
summary: Chemotherapy appears to rapidly destabilise brain function, potentially explaining the 'chemo brain' phenomenon, the study researchers said.

title: Modest Effects of Valacyclovir in Herpes Zoster Eye Disease
summary: Administration of low-dose valacyclovir over a year does not significantly reduce the occurrence of herpes zoster ophthalmicus but shows potential benefits over time.
